name: shopifyapi

metadata.name: ShopifyAPI
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
The ShopifyAPI library allows python developers to programmatically
access the admin section of stores using an ActiveResource like
interface similar the ruby Shopify API gem. The library makes HTTP
requests to Shopify in order to list, create, update, or delete
resources (e.g. Order, Product, Collection).

modules.name: bin.shopify_api
documentation: shopify_api.py wrapper script for running it the source directory
!/usr/bin/env python

methods.name: shopify.session.Session.calculate_hmac
documentation:
Calculate the HMAC of the given parameters in line with Shopify's rules for OAuth authentication.
See http://docs.shopify.com/api/authentication/oauth#verification.

methods.name: shopify.session.Session.__encoded_params_for_signature
documentation: Sort and combine query parameters into a single string, excluding those that should be removed and joining with '&'

methods.name: shopify.resources.customer.Customer.search
documentation:
Search for customers matching supplied query

Args:
   order: Field and direction to order results by (default: last_order_date DESC)
   query: Text to search for customers
   page: Page to show (default: 1)
   limit: Amount of results (default: 50) (maximum: 250)
   fields: comma-separated list of fields to include in the response
Returns:
   A Collection of customers.

methods.name: shopify.resources.refund.Refund.calculate
documentation:
Calculates refund transactions based on line items and shipping.
When you want to create a refund, you should first use the calculate
endpoint to generate accurate refund transactions.

Args:
   order_id: Order ID for which the Refund has to created.
   shipping: Specify how much shipping to refund.
   refund_line_items: A list of line item IDs and quantities to refund.
Returns:
   Unsaved refund record

methods.name: shopify.resources.asset.Asset.find
documentation:
Find an asset by key
E.g.
    shopify.Asset.find('layout/theme.liquid', theme_id=99)

methods.name: shopify.resources.recurring_application_charge.RecurringApplicationCharge.current
documentation:
Returns first RecurringApplicationCharge object with status=active.
If not found, None will be returned.

methods.name: shopify.resources.gift_card.GiftCard.search
documentation:
Search for gift cards matching supplied query

Args:
   order: Field and direction to order results by (default: disabled_at DESC)
   query: Text to search for gift cards
   page: Page to show (default: 1)
   limit: Amount of results (default: 50) (maximum: 250)
   fields: comma-separated list of fields to include in the response
Returns:
   An array of gift cards.

methods.name: shopify.resources.gift_card.GiftCard.add_adjustment
documentation: Create a new Gift Card Adjustment

classes.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.__init__
documentation: If given a Collection object as an argument, inherit its metadata.

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.has_previous_page
documentation: Returns true if the current page has any previous pages before it.

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.has_next_page
documentation: Returns true if the current page has any pages beyond the current position.

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.previous_page
documentation:
Returns the previous page of items.

Args:
    no_cache: If true the page will not be cached.
Returns:
    A PaginatedCollection object with the new data set.

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.next_page
documentation:
Returns the next page of items.

Args:
    no_cache: If true the page will not be cached.
Returns:
    A PaginatedCollection object with the new data set.

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.__iter__
documentation: Iterates through all items, also fetching other pages.

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedCollection.__len__
documentation: If fetched count all the pages.

A subclass of Collection which allows cycling through pages of
data through cursor-based pagination.

:next_page_url contains a url for fetching the next page
:previous_page_url contains a url for fetching the previous page

You can use next_page_url and previous_page_url to fetch the next page
of data by calling Resource.find(from_=page.next_page_url)

classes.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedIterator

methods.name: shopify.collection.PaginatedIterator.__iter__
documentation: Iterate over pages, returning one page at a time.

This class implements an iterator over paginated collections which aims to
be more memory-efficient by not keeping more than one page in memory at a
time.

```pycon
>>> from shopify import Product, PaginatedIterator
>>> for page in PaginatedIterator(Product.find()):
...     for item in page:
...         do_something(item)
...
# every page and the page items are iterated
```
